body: [{"ac":1,"text":"When the last non-terminal child of an epic (parent bead with at least one child) transitions to a terminal state via `ddx bead close`, the epic auto-closes IF policy is \"auto\" (decided in design phase).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":2,"text":"If policy is \"manual\" (current behavior), `ddx bead reap` (or equivalent) lists every epic whose children are all terminal, and `--apply` closes them in a batch.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"New tests exercise: (a) epic with mixed open/closed children stays open; (b) epic with all-closed children either auto-closes or is reported by reap; (c) \"cancelled\" children count as terminal; (d) epics with no children are left alone (no false positives).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"Behavior is documented in `docs/beads.md` (or wherever the bead lifecycle is documented).","kind":"mechanical","verifiable":true},{"ac":5,"text":"`go test ./...` passes for the bead package.","kind":"build-gate","verifiable":true}]

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"implementation_path_unselected: The bead is not executable as written because it leaves the core product choice unresolved: it proposes both auto-close and manual reap/check paths, and it defers the deciding policy to a later design phase. It also leaves terminal-state semantics and closing_commit_sha behavior open, so an implementer cannot start without first narrowing the scope.","fingerprint":"22466983d4893b09874e96a043973070d915ad30e373ef0129f56a6e0ab3992e","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"22466983d4893b09874e96a043973070d915ad30e373ef0129f56a6e0ab3992e"}

[{"ac":1,"text":"When the last non-terminal child of an epic (parent bead with at least one child) transitions to a terminal state via `ddx bead close`, the epic auto-closes IF policy is \"auto\" (decided in design phase).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":2,"text":"If policy is \"manual\" (current behavior), `ddx bead reap` (or equivalent) lists every epic whose children are all terminal, and `--apply` closes them in a batch.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"New tests exercise: (a) epic with mixed open/closed children stays open; (b) epic with all-closed children either auto-closes or is reported by reap; (c) \"cancelled\" children count as terminal; (d) epics with no children are left alone (no false positives).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"Behavior is documented in `docs/beads.md` (or wherever the bead lifecycle is documented).","kind":"mechanical","verifiable":true},{"ac":5,"text":"`go test ./...` passes for the bead package.","kind":"build-gate","verifiable":true}]

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"design decision unresolved: auto-close vs. reap command vs. both: The bead presents three mutually exclusive implementation options without resolving which to pursue. AC#1 explicitly defers to a 'design phase' that has not occurred, making the acceptance criteria conditionally defined. An agent claiming this bead cannot determine whether to extend cascadeAndWalkUp in store.go:1617 to cover epics, add a new 'ddx bead reap' command, or both. The gap in the existing code is locatable and narrow — walkUpClosureCandidate at store.go:1620 skips execution-eligible parents and only counts StatusClosed (not StatusCancelled) as terminal — but the policy decision (auto vs. manual+reap) must be made before implementation begins.","fingerprint":"fd72fb82d692305891fccfb8a10fd7bc313ac8449184cc4e89546dd4fb9e8769","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"fd72fb82d692305891fccfb8a10fd7bc313ac8449184cc4e89546dd4fb9e8769"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"design decision resolved: auto-close vs ddx bead reap: The bead presents two substantially different implementation paths without choosing between them: (1) auto-close triggered on the last child's terminal transition, and (2) a `ddx bead reap` batch command for manual cleanup. AC1 and AC2 are explicitly conditional on a policy described as 'decided in design phase' — a phase that has not occurred. AC3b uses 'either auto-closes or is reported by reap', allowing an agent to satisfy acceptance with whichever path is easier rather than whichever the operator prefers. The filer's own label `ac-quality:needs-refinement` confirms dispatch-readiness has not been reached. No file:line anchor is given for where the child-state propagation hook would be inserted. Lefthook gate is absent from AC.","fingerprint":"37479727195e3bdb0a3d2571c40ca20f87398bc408078291b5dbfc2c28f9a09c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"37479727195e3bdb0a3d2571c40ca20f87398bc408078291b5dbfc2c28f9a09c"}
